description_long: This is a traditional stone water well set on a patch of grassy ground. The well features a circular stone wall with a rough, gray texture, rising to about waist height. Two sturdy, weathered wooden posts are anchored on either side of the well, supporting a horizontal wooden beam. Suspended from the beam is a simple pulley mechanism with a rope and a wooden bucket, used for drawing water. The ground around the well is uneven and natural, with no visible pavement or additional structures. There are no decorative elements or protective barriers, giving the well a utilitarian and rustic appearance.
description: A rustic stone water well with a wooden frame and bucket, set on a grassy patch with a simple pulley mechanism.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone well, wooden posts, horizontal beam, and the bucket hanging over the well opening.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the stone wall and the alignment of the wooden posts and beam.
description_view_2: Alternate front view similar to view 0, with the bucket and pulley mechanism clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the back of the well and the wooden structure, with less visibility of the bucket.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A simple pulley with a rope and wooden bucket is suspended from the horizontal beam above the well.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: A circular stone wall surrounds the well opening, approximately 0.8 meters high; no additional railing or fence is present.
